commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a bounded retry loop around memory-core dream narrative session message reads and a regression test for delayed assistant text.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +45. Total +73 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level but not live: current main reads session messages once after `waitForRun` and falls back when that read lacks assistant text, while the linked report provides production traces of delayed assistant content. I did not run a live cron/Ollama reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer comparison with the broader narrative recovery work, not a narrow repair for automation to apply.

target.title: fix(memory-core): retry narrative message reads
target.body:
Refs #87306

## Summary
- Retry the narrative session message read briefly after a successful run when the first read has no extractable assistant text.
- Keep the existing fallback path unchanged after the bounded settle attempts.
- Add regression coverage for the persistence timing where `waitForRun` succeeds before assistant text is visible through `getSessionMessages`.

## Evidence map
- Changed surface: memory-core dreaming narrative post-run message extraction in `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts`.
- Entry point and callers: `generateAndAppendDreamNarrative`, called by dreaming phase/session orchestration.
- Owner boundary: uses the existing `SubagentSurface.getSessionMessages` API; no SDK, config, provider, or persistence schema change.
- Sibling behavior: timeout, unavailable-model retry, cleanup, and fallback diary paths remain covered by existing tests in the touched file.
- Security/config: no auth, secrets, trust-boundary, dependency, config, or filesystem path changes; the retry is bounded to three extra reads over 500 ms total delay before the existing fallback.

## Verification
- `fnm exec --using 22 node scripts/changed-lanes.mjs --base upstream/main --head HEAD --json`
- `fnm exec --using 22 ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts`
- `fnm exec --using 22 ./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts`
- `fnm exec --using 22 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `fnm exec --using 22 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `memory-core: narrative generation produced no text` warnings could fire, and fallback diary text could be written, when the assistant text existed but was not visible on the first post-run session message read.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at `80f848105e95e2021996997c1d55fb622dea199c` on Node `v22.22.1`, using the patched `generateAndAppendDreamNarrative` implementation with a scripted `SubagentSurface` that exposes assistant text on the second session read.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: After rebasing onto `upstream/main` at `cbc5f277bbb`, ran the commands listed in Verification and this focused after-fix command: `fnm exec --using 22 ./node_modules/.bin/tsx --eval '<script invoking generateAndAppendDreamNarrative and printing DREAMS.md checks>'`.
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local after-fix run:
```text
session_reads=2
warn_produced_no_text=false
contains_delayed_text=true
contains_fallback=false
Delayed assistant diary text reached the session reader.
```
Observed result after fix: The first post-run session read had no assistant text, the second read supplied the delayed assistant diary text, `DREAMS.md` contained that delayed text, the `produced no text` warning was not emitted, and the fallback `A memory trace surfaced` text was not written. The touched-file shard also reported `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 52 passed (52)`, including `generateAndAppendDreamNarrative > waits for persisted assistant text before falling back`.
What was not tested: Live dreaming cron with a real local LLM/Ollama session; broad `pnpm check:changed`/Testbox. The repo-wrapper oxlint command stalled in extension-boundary preparation locally, so touched-file oxlint was run directly against the repo-local binary instead.
